children.text: The song says " I will have to suffer in my life if I were born Black. My destiny is cruel. I carry on my shoulders the chains. The same chains from slavery."
Black women dancing to the song
Visual images by Pancho Fierro. Pancho Fierro was an important Afro-Peruvian painter who used art as a way of representing the daily lives of Black communities during colonial times.

"Being Black in Peru means belonging to history of injustices and suffering that started in 1532 when the first Black enslave people cqame with the Spanirds. Black communities transformed the coast valleys and made other people rich."
"Black people fought for other people to be free. The abolition of slavery in 1855 did not change his dominated condition"
"Their people now are little property land owners. They are facing the capitalist national market without organization to defense themsleves". This
"A society of "castas" only offers them the exploitation of their ancestral languages and the need of whitening".
"My name is Juliana Avalos Gallardo. I am from Guayabo, and I live with my grandma Chicha in El Guayabo. I am in 4th grade of high school, and I dance with the crew Zarandango"
Song " El Negro Antonio". The song narrates the life of Black Antonio who marries another Black women and have children, who are the first generation of Black children to be born free.
"My dad was Antonio Cartagena De la Cruz and my mom was Carmen Rosa Buenano Cotito"
"My mom did not work; she used to have a business. She used to sell animal giblets."
"My mom knew how to read, but she did not know how to write. My dad only knew how to write some numbers, only half of them to count. I really do not know how he learned those things."
"She thought (the mom) that if we learned how to read and write, we would have more opportunities in our lives. Maybe we could have an opportunity better than the ones she had"
"My parents' lives have been really difficult, and we have been carrying that. You gotta see that we have suffered a lot"

"The cooperation did not work because the engineer that we hired always wanted to take a piece. And that is not how it works. And you cannot do anything about it. When you do not understand that stuff, when you are blind in the economies and numbers, they take you out."
